mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations and lowers corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, which expands corporate revenue bases and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant at p > 0.10 in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, a month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates robust labor market expansion that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in over 55% of observations, or the rolling 12-month correlation between payroll changes and forward returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, it forces monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy stances, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the threshold effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in real GDP growth and industrial production signals synchronized macroeconomic expansion that boosts corporate profit margins and equity risk appetite, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and INDPRO uptrend fails to predict positive 20-day returns when the interaction term predictive accuracy drops below 52%, or the signal reverses during financial stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, rising unemployment rates indicate that policy restraint is successfully cooling the economy, which reduces inflation expectations but triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to earnings recession fears.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the mechanism predictive coefficient becomes positive when FEDFUNDS declines.
